intro: Psephellus sergii, also known as Serg's scabious, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the steppes of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus sergii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are employed to treat a range of conditions, including digestive problems and respiratory issues.
